bullets: • **SBI Small Cap Fund** charges an exit load of **1.0%** upon redemption, making it the highest exit load among the three schemes listed in the provided context.
• **SBI ELSS Tax Saver Fund** has an exit load of **0%**, however it carries a mandatory **3-year lock-in period**, meaning redemption is not permitted before the lock-in period ends.
• **SBI Large Cap Fund (Bluechip)** has a tiered exit load structure: **0.25% if redeemed within 0–30 days**, and **0.1% if redeemed within 30–90 days**, as per the provided context data.
• Exit load is a fee charged at the time of **exiting (redeeming)** a mutual fund scheme earlier than a stipulated holding period, and is deducted from the redemption proceeds paid to the investor.

• All three funds carry a **"Very High" risk level** per the provided context, and their respective expense ratios (Direct Plans) are: SBI Small Cap Fund — **0.79%**, SBI ELSS Tax Saver Fund — **0.99%**, SBI Large Cap Fund — **0.84%**.

• The exit load and expense ratio data referenced above are sourced from: [https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/sbi-small-cap-fund-direct-plan-growth-3603](https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/sbi-small-cap-fund-direct-plan-growth-3603) and [https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/sbi-bluechip-fund-direct-growth-3046](https://www.indmoney.com/mutual-funds/sbi-bluechip-fund-direct-growth-3046).
